content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible, and the car appears to be on the left side of the track heading into a right-hand bend.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 102, target = (102 + 5) % 120 = 107
- Waypoint 107: x=1.0942, y=1.6655
- Car: x=0.9413, y=1.6024
- Bearing = atan2(1.6655 - 1.6024, 1.0942 - 0.9413) = atan2(0.0631, 0.1529) ≈ 22.5°
- Current heading: -70.55°
- Delta = 22.5° - (-70.55°) = +93.05° → very sharp left needed

Wait, that seems too large. Let me reconsider — the car is heading south (-70°) and the waypoints ahead are going northward (increasing y), so the delta is large as expected. This is the bottom of the loop turning back north.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Car is at the bottom of the southward loop and waypoints ahead curve sharply back northward; large positive delta of ~93° means maximum left steering at low speed to navigate the tight turn.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track loops sharply south then back northeast around waypoints 95-110",
    "Positive delta means steer left (positive steering_angle)",
    "At delta >50°, use speed=0.75-1.0 and steering=+20 to +25°",
    "Car heading around -70° (mostly south), needs to swing toward northeast",
    "After southward section, waypoints loop back east then north",
    "Avoid overcorrecting — once delta < 5°, reduce steering to 0°",
    "Progress at 85-86% — in tight bottom turn of loop, stay slow and controlled",
    "Delta very large (~93°) at bottom of loop — use max left steer at 0.75 speed",
    "Car is right of center (is_left_of_center=false) — track curves left ahead",
    "Once heading swings past 0° (east), reduce steering to avoid overshoot"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [0.832, 0.868, 0.903, 0.941],
    "y": [1.778, 1.714, 1.660, 1.602],
    "yaw": [-85.2, -79.1, -74.4, -70.5]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 93.05,
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": 25.0
}
```
